papers.title_en: FlashMemory-DeepSeek-V4: Lightning Index Ultra-Long Context via Lookahead Sparse Attention
papers.authors: Yan Wang, Qifan Zhang, Jiachen Yu, Tian Liang, Dongyang Ma, Xiang Hu, Zibo Lin, Chunyang Li, Zhichao Wang, Jia Li, Yujiu Yang, Haitao Mi, Dong Yu
papers.evidence: 针对LLM高效推理的KV缓存优化
papers.tldr: 常规大语言模型解码时需维护完整KV缓存，在超长上下文场景下造成严重GPU内存瓶颈。本文提出Lookahead Sparse Attention，利用基于DeepSeek-V4的神经内存索引器主动预测未来上下文需求，仅保留查询关键的KV块。通过无主干解耦训练策略独立训练索引器，避免加载主干模型。实验表明，该方法将物理KV缓存压缩至全上下文基线的13.5%，下游准确率平均提升0.6%，在500K极端长度下缓存开销降低超90%，实现了高效且稳定的超长上下文推理。
papers.abstract_en: Conventional LLMs keep the full KV cache loaded during decoding, causing a severe GPU memory bottleneck for ultra-long context serving. In this report, we propose Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA), a novel inference paradigm powered by a Neural Memory Indexer built upon the DeepSeek-V4 architecture. Rather than passively attending to all historical tokens, LSA proactively predicts future context demands and preserves only the query-critical KV chunks in the GPU memory. Crucially, we instantiate this architecture via a backbone-free decoupled training strategy. By formulating the indexer as a standard dual-encoder architecture, we train it independently using standard retrieval training frameworks without ever loading the massive backbone model into GPU memory.   We demonstrate that this "less is more" paradigm significantly maximizes serving efficiency while acting as an effective attention denoiser in tasks that rely on long-term global memory. Across primary long-context evaluation suites (e.g., LongBench-v2, LongMemEval, and RULER), FM-DS-V4 compresses the average physical KV cache footprint down to merely 13.5% of the full-context baseline, while consistently preserving or slightly elevating downstream accuracy (+0.6% absolute margin on average). Crucially, at extreme 500K scales, FlashMemory suppresses the physical KV cache overhead by over 90% without destabilizing the backbone's core reasoning capacities.

papers.title_en: FlashMemory-DeepSeek-V4: Lightning Index Ultra-Long Context via Lookahead Sparse Attention
papers.authors: Yan Wang, Qifan Zhang, Jiachen Yu, Tian Liang, Dongyang Ma, Xiang Hu, Zibo Lin, Chunyang Li, Zhichao Wang, Miao Peng, Nuo Chen, Jia Li, Yujiu Yang, Haitao Mi, Dong Yu
papers.tldr: 传统LLM在解码时加载全量KV缓存，导致超长上下文服务出现GPU内存瓶颈。为此，提出Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA) 推理范式，基于DeepSeek-V4构建神经记忆索引器，主动预测未来上下文需求，仅保留查询关键KV块。采用解耦训练策略，索引器作为独立双编码器无需加载大模型。在LongBench-v2等基准上，物理KV缓存压缩至13.5%，精度平均提升0.6%；500K长度下开销降低90%以上。该范式最大化服务效率，同时作为注意力去噪器增强长程全局记忆任务性能。
papers.abstract_en: Conventional LLMs keep the full KV cache loaded during decoding, causing a severe GPU memory bottleneck for ultra-long context serving. In this report, we propose Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA), a novel inference paradigm powered by a Neural Memory Indexer built upon the DeepSeek-V4 architecture. Rather than passively attending to all historical tokens, LSA proactively predicts future context demands and preserves only the query-critical KV chunks in the GPU memory. Crucially, we instantiate this architecture via a backbone-free decoupled training strategy. By formulating the indexer as a standard dual-encoder architecture, we train it independently using standard retrieval training frameworks without ever loading the massive backbone model into GPU memory.   We demonstrate that this "less is more" paradigm significantly maximizes serving efficiency while acting as an effective attention denoiser in tasks that rely on long-term global memory. Across primary long-context evaluation suites (e.g., LongBench-v2, LongMemEval, and RULER), FM-DS-V4 compresses the average physical KV cache footprint down to merely 13.5% of the full-context baseline, while consistently preserving or slightly elevating downstream accuracy (+0.6% absolute margin on average). Crucially, at extreme 500K scales, FlashMemory suppresses the physical KV cache overhead by over 90% without destabilizing the backbone's core reasoning capacities.

papers.title_en: Last But Not Least: Boundary Attention CalibratiON for Multimodal KV Cache Compression
papers.authors: Tianhao Chen, Yuheng Wu, Kelu Yao, Xiaogang Xu, Xiaobin Hu, Dongman Lee
papers.evidence: 多模态LLM的KV缓存压缩降低解码延迟
papers.tldr: 多模态大语言模型在长视觉上下文下KV缓存过大、解码延迟高。现有压缩方法依赖观察窗口注意力，易稀释稀疏视觉证据并丢弃关键token。本文提出BACON，利用最后查询注意力校准窗口注意力，并通过层内一致性和层间持久性抑制噪声。在多种基准上，激进压缩下平均提升7.5%，最高30.9%。该方法即插即用，显著改善了多模态KV缓存压缩质量。
papers.abstract_en: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong vision-language reasoning, but long visual contexts enlarge the KV cache and increase decoding latency. Existing compression methods rely on observation window attention for stable token-importance estimation, yet this aggregation can dilute sparse visual evidence and discard answer-critical tokens under aggressive compression. Therefore, we identify last-query attention as a complementary source for recovering such evidence, but its answer-irrelevant signals can mislead retention. We propose BACON, a plug-and-play method that calibrates observation window attention with last-query evidence and suppresses isolated noise via intra-layer coherence and inter-layer persistence. Across diverse benchmarks, models, budgets, and compression methods, BACON improves multimodal KV compression by 7.5% on average under the most aggressive budget, with gains up to 30.9%.

papers.title_en: From Rigid to Dynamic: Entropy-Guided Adaptive Inference for Long-Context LLMs
papers.authors: Zhanchao Xu, Haoyang Li, Qingfa Xiao, Fei Teng, Chen Jason Zhang, Lei Chen, Qing Li
papers.evidence: 基于熵的自适应KV缓存压缩，提升长上下文大语言模型推理效率
papers.tldr: 长上下文LLM推理中，现有稀疏注意力和KV缓存压缩方法采用固定模式，忽略了注意力头间的行为差异。本文发现存在刚性头和动态头两种熵模式，其分布依赖于上下文。为此提出EntropyInfer，无需训练，在预填充阶段利用注意力熵为每个头动态分配计算，并在解码阶段利用生成令牌压缩KV缓存。实验表明，该方法在Llama、Qwen等模型上相比SnapKV等基线获得最高2.39倍端到端加速，且质量损失极小。
papers.abstract_en: Existing sparse attention and KV cache compression methods for long-context LLM inference typically apply fixed sparsity patterns or uniform budgets across all attention heads, overlooking the substantial variation in attention behavior among heads and contexts. We observe two distinct entropy patterns among attention heads: Rigid Heads, whose entropy stays near zero across input segments, and Dynamic Heads, whose entropy fluctuates significantly. Crucially, the distribution of these types is context-dependent and cannot be predetermined offline. We therefore propose EntropyInfer, a training-free framework that uses attention entropy to adaptively allocate compute at the granularity of individual heads and segments during prefilling. For decoding, we introduce a latent KV cache compression scheme that leverages generated output tokens, rather than prefill tokens alone, to identify and retain the most critical cache entries. Extensive experiments on Llama, Qwen and openPangu model series show that EntropyInfer consistently outperforms baselines including SnapKV, AdaKV, and CritiPrefill, achieving up to 2.39$\times$ end-to-end speedup beyond 100k tokens with minimal quality degradation compared to full attention. The code is released in https://github.com/SHA-4096/EntropyInfer.

papers.authors: Ang Li, Sean McLeish, Haozhe Chen, Nimit Kalra, Zaiqian Chen, Artem Gazizov, Venkata Anoop Suhas Kumar Morisetty, Bhavya Kailkhura, Harshitha Menon, Zhuang Liu, Brian R. Bartoldson, Tom Goldstein, Sanae Lotfi, Micah Goldblum, Pavel Izmailov
papers.evidence: 针对LLM高效推理的KV缓存压缩
papers.tldr: 4、1:8、1:16压缩比连续预训练。LCLM在通用任务性能、压缩速度和峰值内存间实现了更优的帕累托前沿，可作为长时智能体的高效骨干。
papers.abstract_en: Long-context language model inference is bottlenecked by memory, as the KV cache grows with context length. Recent techniques to compress the KV cache fall short: they either degrade model quality substantially or require considerable time and compute to compress a single long prompt. Furthermore, many methods require the input to fit within the target model's context window, and are generally incompatible with modern production inference engines. Encoder-decoder compressors, which map a long token sequence to a shorter sequence of latent embeddings consumed by a decoder, are an appealing alternative in principle. However, existing approaches are not competitive with KV cache compression on the accuracy-efficiency frontier. In this work, we revisit encoder-decoder compression and close this gap. We first perform an architecture search, pre-training many variants from scratch to determine how best to design and train encoder-decoder compressors. Guided by our findings, we continually pre-train a family of 0.6B-encoder, 4B-decoder models on over 350B tokens each, at compression ratios of 1:4, 1:8, and 1:16. We introduce Latent Context Language Models (LCLMs), a family of compressors that improve the Pareto frontier across general-task performance, compression speed, and peak memory usage. We demonstrate that LCLMs serve as efficient backbones for long-horizon agents, letting the agent skim through a compressed long context and adaptively expand relevant segments on demand.

papers.authors: Albert Gong, Annabelle Michael Carrell, Raaz Dwivedi, Lester Mackey
papers.evidence: 高效注意力近似加速LLM推理，含KV缓存压缩
papers.tldr: 因果注意力近似缺乏从非因果到因果的转换保证，导致长序列语言建模存在资源瓶颈。为此提出Express方法，将非因果近似转换为因果近似并保持误差界，结合Thinformer实现log^{3/2}(n)/s的近似误差，仅需O(s)内存与O(s^2 log^2(n))压缩开销。基于Triton的高效I/O实现显著超越FlashAttention 2。该方法同时解决了长上下文预填充、KV缓存压缩、长形式内存受限解码和计算受限解码四个关键瓶颈，提升了语言建模的效率和可扩展性。
papers.abstract_en: We introduce a new tool, Express, for converting a non-causal attention approximation into a causal approximation with matching approximation guarantees. When combined with the state-of-the-art Thinformer approximation, Express improves upon the best known causal attention guarantees, delivering $\log^{3/2}(n)/s$ approximation error with only $O(s)$ memory and $O(s^2 \log^2(n))$ compression overhead for a sequence of length $n$. We pair these developments with an efficient I/O-aware Triton implementation, demonstrate substantial speedups over FlashAttention 2, and use Express to overcome four resource bottlenecks in the language modeling pipeline: long-context prefill, KV cache compression, long-form memory-constrained decoding, and long-form compute-constrained decoding.

papers.title_en: Efficient On-Device Diffusion LLM Inference with Mobile NPU
papers.authors: Tuowei Wang, Yanfan Sun, Ju Ren
papers.evidence: 设备端扩散LLM推理与NPU加速，KV缓存复用优化，高效推理
papers.tldr: 扩散大语言模型(dLLM)通过并行去噪加速生成，适合移动设备，但智能手机NPU上存在计算量巨大、token提交导致工作负载缩小、token修订使KV缓存复用复杂、NPU地址空间有限带来重映射和数据传输开销等问题。本文提出llada.cpp，首个面向智能手机NPU的dLLM推理框架，通过多块推测解码、双路径渐进修订和交换优化内存运行时三项技术，将LLaDA-8B生成延迟相比CPU基线降低17-42倍，同时保持生成质量。
papers.abstract_en: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) accelerate generation by denoising multiple tokens in parallel, making them attractive for latency-sensitive mobile inference. However, repeated denoising introduces substantial computation on smartphones. Mobile neural processing units (NPUs) offer high-throughput dense matrix computation, but efficiently exploiting them remains challenging: token commitment shrinks per-block effective workloads, token revision complicates KV cache reuse, and limited NPU-visible address space incurs costly remapping and data transfer overheads.   In this paper, we propose llada.cpp, the first NPU-aware inference framework for accelerating dLLMs on smartphones. llada.cpp aligns block-wise dLLM inference with the execution characteristics of mobile NPUs through three techniques. (1) Multi-Block Speculative Decoding fills the shrinking workload in late-stage current-block decoding with speculative future-block tokens. (2) Dual-Path Progressive Revision keeps committed tokens revisable until stable and refreshes unstable tokens through a CPU-side path without stalling dense NPU execution. (3) Swap-Optimized Memory Runtime compacts NPU-visible address layouts and overlaps data staging with NPU computation to reduce remapping and transfer overheads. We implement llada.cpp as an end-to-end framework and evaluate it across diverse hardware platforms and dLLM workloads. llada.cpp reduces LLaDA-8B generation latency by 17x-42x over the CPU baseline with prefix KV cache reuse, while preserving generation quality.

papers.title_en: Think Less, Act Early: Reinforced Latent Reasoning with Early Exit in Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.evidence: 早期退出减少VLA模型推理开销
papers.tldr: 现有视觉-语言-动作模型依赖显式思维链推理，计算成本高且易错误累积。本文提出AVA-VLA框架，将推理建模为潜在变量序列，无需显式文本生成；并引入强化学习去噪机制优化推理轨迹，结合早期退出策略动态调整推理深度。在LIBERO基准上实现6倍推理加速和98.3%平均成功率，兼顾效率与长程稳定性。
papers.abstract_en: Existing Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models predominantly rely on explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning to bridge perception and action. While effective, this paradigm suffers from high computational costs and error propagation in multi-step tasks. In this paper, we propose Adaptive Variable Alignment VLA (AVA-VLA), a novel Latent Reasoning VLA framework that models reasoning as a sequence of unobservable latent variables, bypassing the need for explicit text generation. However, latent trajectories are inherently susceptible to noise interference and misalignment with downstream objectives. To address this, we introduce a Reinforcement Learning-based Denoising mechanism that treats latent state generation as a sequential decision process, optimizing reasoning trajectories via task-level rewards. Furthermore, we incorporate an Early-Exit Strategy that adaptively terminates reasoning based on state confidence, enabling a dynamic trade-off between depth and efficiency. Extensive experiments on embodied decision benchmarks demonstrate that AVA-VLA achieves a 6x inference speedup over explicit CoT methods while attaining a 98.3% average success rate on LIBERO, improving both efficiency and long-horizon stability over full-reasoning baselines.

papers.title_en: Mean-Field Parallel Decoding for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
papers.authors: Tamim Zoabi, Ameen Ali, Liran Ringel, Lior Wolf
papers.evidence: 离散扩散语言模型的并行解码，与高效LLM推理相关
papers.tldr: 离散扩散语言模型并行解码时，独立按边际置信度选择令牌易产生不兼容配置。本文提出无训练平均场并行解码框架，通过固定点迭代更新每个掩码位置的提交分数，利用模型预测分布的成对交互抑制冲突，在单次前向传播中实现更多令牌的协调提交。该方法轻量、无需额外模型或重训练，在推理与代码生成基准上持续改进质量-延迟权衡。
papers.abstract_en: Discrete diffusion language models enable parallel token generation, offering a pathway to low-latency decoding. However, selecting tokens independently by marginal confidence limits effective parallelism: tokens that appear reliable in isolation can form incompatible configurations when several positions are updated at once. We introduce a training-free decoding framework that coordinates these parallel updates. At each forward pass, the method assigns a commit score to each masked position and refines these scores using pairwise interactions derived from the model's predictive distributions. A variational relaxation yields a simple fixed-point update that suppresses conflicting simultaneous commitments within a single forward pass. This mechanism allows the decoder to commit more tokens in parallel while maintaining competitive generation quality. The method is lightweight, requires no auxiliary model or retraining, and drops into existing diffusion decoding pipelines without modification. Experiments on reasoning and code-generation benchmarks show consistent improvements in the quality-latency trade-off.

papers.title_en: RoVE: Rotary Value Embeddings Attention for Relative Position-dependent Value Pathways
papers.authors: Alejandro García-Castellanos, Maurice Weiler, Erik J Bekkers
papers.evidence: 旋转值嵌入注意力，高效注意力机制
papers.tldr: RoPE虽然实现了注意力分数的位置相对性，但值通路仍缺乏位置感知。本文提出RoVE，通过旋转键和值使值通路对位置敏感，将RoPE注意力转化为注意力卷积。实验表明，RoVE在少样本上下文学习、分布外困惑度和长上下文检索任务上均优于RoPE，尤其在长程聚合任务中提升显著。该工作统一了计算机视觉、机器人学和现代LLM架构中的相关操作。
papers.abstract_en: Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) make attention scores position-relative but leave the value pathway position-blind: the message sent by a value token is the same regardless of its distance from the query. We propose RoVE, a parameter-free modification that makes values position-sensitive by rotating them simultaneously with keys, and show that it turns RoPE attention into attentive convolution. This new perspective unifies several independent formulations of the same operation across computer vision, robotics, and modern LLM architectures. Trained 124M and 354M GPT-2 models show consistent empirical gains over RoPE on few-shot in-context learning, out-of-distribution perplexity, and long-context retrieval, with the clearest improvements on tasks that require long-range aggregation.

papers.title_en: Task-Aware Structured Memory for Dynamic Multi-modal In-Context Learning
papers.authors: Zhirui Chen, Ziwei Chen, Ling Shao
papers.evidence: 面向多模态上下文学习的任务感知结构化KV缓存压缩
papers.tldr: 多模态大模型依赖上下文学习但受限于有限上下文窗口和KV缓存增长。现有记忆压缩方法引入偏差、破坏语义结构且记忆静态。TASM提出训练无关框架，通过任务向量引导压缩、语义感知token合并（二分图匹配）和分层记忆（核心记忆+潜在库）实现动态检索。实验表明在强压缩下保持高性能，平衡效率与适应性。
papers.abstract_en: Multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) depend on in-context learning (ICL) for rapid task adaptation, but their scalability is severely limited by finite context windows and the growing cost of key-value (KV) caches in long multi-modal sequences. Existing memory compression approaches typically rely on rigid token removal or sample-dependent importance estimation, which introduces bias, disrupts semantic structure, particularly for visual representations, and yields static memories that cannot adapt to new queries. We introduce TASM (Task-Aware Structured Memory), a training-free framework that addresses these limitations through task-aware, structure-preserving, and dynamically accessible memory construction. TASM employs task-vector guided compression to replace sample-specific signals with a task-level direction that captures shared relevance across demonstrations. To preserve the underlying manifold, it applies semantics-aware token merging via bipartite graph matching, aggregating tokens without destructive pruning. Finally, TASM structures memory into a hierarchy comprising a compact Core Memory and a Latent Bank, facilitating query-adaptive dynamic retrieval. Evaluations confirm TASM maintains high performance under heavy compression, effectively balancing efficiency with adaptability.

papers.title_en: Follow the Latent Roadmap: Navigating Revocable Decoding for Diffusion LLMs with Anchor Tokens
papers.authors: Yizhen Yao, Qinglin Zhu, Runcong Zhao, Xiangxiang Dai, Yanzheng Xiang, Yulan He, Lin Gui
papers.evidence: 可撤销解码中的验证机制
papers.tldr: 扩散大语言模型(dLLMs)并行生成面临解码速度与质量权衡，可恢复解码策略存在错误传播和局部错误强化问题。提出无训练框架ASRD，利用锚点标记在嵌入空间解耦解码上下文，通过锚点引导生成和锚点扰动验证两个机制缓解错误。在数学和编程基准上，准确率提升达6.4%，推理吞吐量加速至7.2倍。该工作为dLLMs的高效可靠解码提供了新路径。
papers.abstract_en: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) offer a promising avenue for parallel generation but face a trade-off between decoding speed and quality. While revocable decoding strategies attempt to mitigate errors by verifying and remasking tokens, they typically operate within a mixed-quality context. This leads to two critical failures: \textit{Error Propagation}, where new tokens absorb toxic information from erroneous context, and \textit{Local Error Reinforcement}, where errors mutually reinforce each other to evade detection. To alleviate these challenges, we propose ASRD (Anchor Supervised Revocable Decoding), a training-free framework that operates within the embedding space. ASRD explicitly decouples the decoding context into trusted \textit{Anchor Tokens}, which are identified via temporal consistency, and uncertain candidates. Leveraging a dynamic Anchor Tokens Cache, we introduce two complementary mechanisms: (1) Anchor-Guided Generation, which injects entropy-weighted anchor signals into masked positions to implicitly rectify attention toward the reliable global skeleton; and (2) Anchor-Perturbed Verification, which applies orthogonal perturbations to uncertain candidate tokens, destabilizing and remasking errors driven by fragile local consensus. Extensive experiments on math and coding benchmarks demonstrate that ASRD outperforms recent remasking baselines, achieving accuracy improvements of up to 6.4\% while accelerating inference throughput by up to 7.2$\times$.
